extra.reasoning: The property requires globally(opted_out implies not sent). Transition t5 (on send_attempt if opted_out and ticks>=5: sent := true) directly sets sent=true while opted_out is true, which would violate the property if reachable. It is reachable: (1) optout sets opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=0; (2-6) five tick events drive ticks 0->1->2->3->4->5 (each setting sent=false harmlessly, and ticks caps at 5); (7) send_attempt now satisfies t5's guard (opted_out=true AND ticks>=5), so t5 fires and sets sent=true. The resulting state has opted_out=true and sent=true, so opted_out implies not sent evaluates to true implies false = false. Note that on this final send_attempt both t3 (guard opted_out) and t5 (guard opted_out and ticks>=5) are enabled; since t5 is a valid enabled transition, an execution can select it, and the property must hold on EVERY reachable execution. The 7-event witness is within the 9-event bound.
